title: NASA names crew for Artemis 3 mission to test lunar landers
summary: NASA has selected the crew for the Artemis 3 mission, which will test lunar lander prototypes in low Earth orbit. The mission aims to prepare for future lunar landings.

title: Warren AI™ Risk analysis engine Is now Live: Building The Risk Intelligence Infrastructure Layer for The Space Economy
summary: Warren AI has launched its Risk analysis engine to support the growing space economy. The engine aims to provide risk intelligence infrastructure for satellite launches, commercial space stations, and space manufacturing.

title: BlackSky to accelerate broad-area collection work under NRO contract modification
summary: The National Reconnaissance Office (NRO) modified a contract with BlackSky Technology to speed up the development of AROS broad-area-collection satellites. This effort aims to advance the satellite's flight capabilities.

title: New Glenn forced an explosive rewrite for NASA’s plans to build a moon base
summary: NASA's plans to build a lunar base were significantly altered due to the delays and uncertainties surrounding Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. The agency's 'Ignition' event in March outlined a new direction for human spaceflight, but these plans had to be revised.

title: Iceye raises 1 billion euros to expand SAR satellite systems
summary: Iceye, a Finnish company specializing in radar imaging satellites, has secured over 1 billion euros in funding, valuing the company at more than 10 billion euros. The capital will be used to expand its Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) satellite systems.

title: Isar Aerospace raises 270 million euros for global launch expansion
summary: Isar Aerospace secures 270 million euros ($312 million) in funding to expand its global launch operations. The European launch company is preparing for its next launch attempt with the new capital.

title: Commercial launch providers shortlisted for China’s low-cost cargo spacecraft
summary: China has shortlisted four commercial rocket companies to launch a new low-cost cargo spacecraft for its space station. This move expands the role of private firms in the country's space logistics.

title: Let’s procure in-orbit services like Earth observation data and services
summary: MizarVision, a Chinese company, gained attention for its open-source Earth observation data analysis over the Middle East, revealing images of F-35s 24 hours before Operation Epic. The article emphasizes the importance of procuring in-orbit services like Earth observation data.

title: FCC lets Amazon Leo miss deployment deadline with temporary spectrum penalty
summary: The FCC has granted Amazon's Project Kuiper a temporary reprieve from its deployment deadline for its broadband satellite constellation, but with a spectrum penalty. This could give competitors like SpaceX an advantage in spectrum priority.

title: Russia is jamming GPS from space
summary: Russia is reportedly jamming GPS signals from space, posing a significant risk to American GPS systems. This follows concerns raised in a 2024 SpaceNews opinion article about potential high-impact GPS interference.

title: These record-breaking black hole winds could create a category 79 hurricane on Earth
summary: Astronomers discovered a quasar with record-breaking black hole winds traveling at 30% the speed of light. These winds could theoretically create a Category 79 hurricane on Earth.

title: Thruster breakthrough? New 2-in-1 propulsion system is about to get an in-space test
summary: A new 2-in-1 propulsion system that uses a single monopropellant fuel for both electrical and chemical thrusters is set for an in-space test, potentially reducing mass and complexity for Mars missions.

title: Do galaxies have a 'kill switch' that makes them stop growing?
summary: A recent study suggests that galaxies have a mechanism that halts their growth beyond a certain threshold, regardless of available raw materials. The research aims to uncover what triggers this 'kill switch' in galactic development.

title: NASA reveals Artemis 3 astronaut crew
summary: NASA announced the astronaut crew for the Artemis 3 mission, which will test docking procedures in Earth orbit ahead of a planned moon landing in 2028.

title: 'This is actually taking a page out of the Communist Party playbook': New White House proposal could deny scientists funding based on their political opinions
summary: A new White House proposal could deny scientists funding based on their political opinions, which critics argue undermines expertise by prioritizing political appointees. The policy is seen as a threat to the U.S. scientific ecosystem and global scientific collaboration.

title: Astronaut captures snake-like auroras from a SpaceX Dragon | Space photo of the day for June 9, 2026
summary: A NASA astronaut captured a stunning image of snake-like auroras from a SpaceX Dragon spacecraft while in orbit. The photo was featured as the Space photo of the day on June 9, 2026.

title: Why Steven Spielberg's 'Disclosure Day' feels like the most exciting sci-fi release of the year
summary: Steven Spielberg's upcoming 'Disclosure Day' is generating excitement as a fresh sci-fi release, offering an original story with 'X-Files'-style conspiracy themes. The film is seen as a refreshing break from mainstream mega-franchises.

title: How should we handle alien detection in a world of AI, deepfakes and social media? This committee is writing the rulebook
summary: A committee is developing guidelines for handling alien detection in the age of AI, deepfakes, and social media. The effort aims to ensure transparency if real extraterrestrial life is discovered.

title: Watch the Jupiter-Venus conjunction unfold live online today
summary: A live online event will showcase the Jupiter-Venus conjunction on June 9 at 3:30 p.m. (1930 GMT). The livestream offers a rare celestial viewing opportunity.

title: Flight Dynamics Research Facility Characteristics
summary: The Flight Dynamics Research Facility (FDRF) is a large subsonic wind tunnel with a vertical test section designed for flight dynamics research, including stability, controllability, free-fall, and aircraft spin testing.

title: Artemis III Crew Announced
summary: NASA announced the crew for the Artemis III mission, including astronauts Andre Douglas, Luca Parmitano, Randy Bresnik, and Frank Rubio. The mission will test integrated operations between the Orion spacecraft and other systems in low Earth orbit.

title: La NASA avanza hacia la misión Artemis III en 2027 y anuncia a su tripulación
summary: NASA is advancing toward the Artemis III mission planned for 2027, announcing the crew members for this complex human spaceflight. The mission aims to return humans to the Moon as part of the Artemis program.

title: NASA Marches Toward Artemis III Mission in 2027, Names Crew Members
summary: NASA announced the crew members for the Artemis III mission, which aims to return humans to the Moon in 2027. The mission will conduct critical tests in Earth orbit to prepare for Artemis IV.

title: June 2026 Satellite Puzzler
summary: A satellite image from June 2026 is presented as a puzzle, challenging viewers to identify its location and explain its significance. The image's unique features or context make it intriguing for analysis.

title: NASA Knows: What Is Mass Distribution?
summary: NASA explains the concept of mass distribution and its applications in various fields like astronomy, aircraft design, and planetary rotation. The article is aimed at students in grades 5-8.

title: How NASA Science and Artemis Are Shaping the 2026 FIFA World Cup
summary: NASA is showcasing space science and engineering at the 2026 FIFA World Cup's FIFA Fan Festival in Houston, highlighting research from the International Space Station and Artemis missions. The exhibit aims to educate soccer fans about the benefits of space exploration and its applications on Earth.

title: Supersonic!
summary: NASA's experimental X-59 aircraft achieved supersonic flight for the first time on June 5, 2026, reaching Mach 1.1 during an 81-minute test flight. The milestone paves the way for demonstrating its quiet supersonic capabilities later in the year.

title: NASA’s INCUS Satellites Progress Toward Launch
summary: NASA's INCUS mission, consisting of three satellites, has completed testing at Blue Canyon Technologies in preparation for a late May 2026 launch. The mission aims to survey the dynamics of tropical storms for the first time from space.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). This crewed mission is part of the agency's plan to return humans to the Moon.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase teams are preparing Booster 20 for cryogenic testing ahead of the upcoming Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: The upcoming week includes space launches from China, Rocket Lab, and multiple Starlink missions. Flights are scheduled from California, Virginia, and Florida.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month effort to restore communication. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions despite the loss of the New Glenn rocket during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on future objectives following the anomaly.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress on the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This development marks a key milestone in advancing the spacecraft's reusability capabilities.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: Space Launch Delta 45 (SLD 45) is evaluating damage and potential future risks related to an anomaly. The Eastern Range, operated by SLD 45, has been cleared for operations.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparation for the mission, despite ongoing uncertainties about timelines and landers.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36, aiming for a return to flight by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week. Five missions are planned, with SpaceX and Chinese providers leading the manifest.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years of achievements with a commemorative booklet highlighting 50 hallmark milestones. The publication reflects on the agency's contributions to space exploration and science over five decades.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is leading this effort with innovative approaches.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space activities.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test bake-out is described. The image suggests a thermal or deployment test for a parachute system.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is preparing the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover's 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The mission, launching in 2028, aims to search for signs of past or present life on Mars while adhering to strict planetary protection protocols.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo More Time to Launch
summary: The FCC has extended the deadline for Amazon's Project Kuiper to launch at least half of its satellite constellation, while imposing conditions to expedite the process.

title: Artemis III Astronauts Will Meet SpaceX, Blue Origin Landers in Earth Orbit
summary: NASA announced that SpaceX and Blue Origin will each provide a lunar lander for a 2027 Earth orbit test mission, preparing for future Moon landings. The Artemis III astronauts will meet these landers in orbit as part of the mission.

title: Reflex Aerospace Taps Arkadia Space for 2027 Mission
summary: Reflex Aerospace has selected Arkadia Space to supply a propulsion system for its upcoming satellite mission scheduled for launch in 2027. This partnership highlights the growing collaboration within the space industry to advance satellite technology.

title: ICEYE, Isar Aerospace Announce Significant Funding Rounds
summary: ICEYE, a Finnish Earth observation company, raised €450M ($520M) in a Series F funding round at a €10B ($12B) valuation. Isar Aerospace was also mentioned in the funding announcement.

title: Quantum Space To Go Public Via SPAC
summary: Quantum Space announced plans to go public via a SPAC merger to accelerate scaling and access public markets. The company emphasized the need for capital and speed to achieve its goals.

title: NewOrbit Raises $18.5M to Build VLEO Spacecraft at Scale
summary: NewOrbit has raised $18.5 million to develop and scale its Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) spacecraft. The company plans to launch its first VLEO-capable NEO-1 satellite in 2028.

title: Juno Propulsion Raises $1.4M Pre-Seed for RDE Flight
summary: Juno Propulsion secured $1.4 million in pre-seed funding to develop its first flight-ready rotating detonation engine (RDE), Project Iris, with a demo mission planned for 2027.

title: Mass Is the Easy Part, Coordination Is The Hard Part
summary: Ukraine's use of cheap drones has forced the U.S. to adopt mass-produced, autonomous, and disposable systems to counter adversaries. The shift highlights the changing economics of modern conflict.

title: AstroForge Completes its DeepSpace-2 Spacecraft
summary: AstroForge has completed its DeepSpace-2 spacecraft, which aims to demonstrate the company's asteroid rendezvous capability. This mission sets the foundation for future asteroid mining efforts planned before the end of the decade.

title: Apex Raises $200M At $2.3B Valuation
summary: Apex has raised $200 million in a funding round, valuing the company at $2.3 billion. This marks the third time the company has secured a $200 million round in approximately 14 months.

title: Iceye’s Valuation Soars to 10 Billion Euro With New Raise
summary: Iceye's valuation has surged to 10 billion euros ($12 billion) following a new funding round led by General Atlantic. The company raised 450 million euros ($520 million) in the latest investment.

title: New Astroscale Initiative Looks to Improve Data for Spacecraft Reentry Studies
summary: Astroscale is launching a new initiative to enhance spacecraft reentry studies while safeguarding sensitive data for commercial operators. The company will lead the Atmospheric Impact project.

title: NRO Awards BlackSky Multi-Million Dollar Modification to Accelerate AROS Development
summary: The National Reconnaissance Office (NRO) awarded BlackSky a multi-million dollar contract modification to accelerate the development of its AROS multi-spectral Earth observation satellites. This initiative aims to enhance broad area Earth observation capabilities.

title: Integrasys and Safran Electronics & Defense Invest in New German Facilities
summary: Integrasys and Safran Electronics & Defense are investing in new facilities in Germany, a key market for space and defense. The move highlights growing interest in the German sector.

title: Quantum Space to Go Public in SPAC Deal
summary: Quantum Space, led by former NASA Administrator Jim Bridenstine, plans to go public via a SPAC merger with Inflection Point Acquisition. This deal values Quantum Space at an undisclosed amount.

title: Finland’s Voimatel to Integrate Eutelsat LEO for Arctic Communications
summary: Voimatel will integrate Eutelsat's Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) connectivity into its Arctic communications services. The partnership aims to enhance coverage in the remote Arctic region.

title: KSAT to Lead European Pollution-Focused Poseidon EO Mission
summary: KSAT will lead the European Poseidon Earth observation mission focused on tracking pollution using optical and radar-based satellite technology. The project was announced under an award issued on Monday.

title: FCC Kicks Off Review of Amazon/Globalstar Deal
summary: The FCC has initiated a review process for Amazon's acquisition of Globalstar, with comments due by July 6. The deal was announced in April.

title: Inside the State of the Satellite Industry 2026 Report with SIA President Tom Stroup
summary: The Satellite Industry Association (SIA) releases its 2026 report, highlighting projections and demand for satellite broadband and direct-to-device services. SIA President Tom Stroup discusses industry trends and hype in the space sector.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo 50% Deployment Waiver, With Conditions on Spectrum Priority
summary: The FCC granted Amazon a 50% deployment waiver for its Project Kuiper constellation, allowing it to launch half of its satellites by the end of July with conditions on spectrum priority. The decision comes with regulatory constraints on spectrum usage.
